brief_notes: Impressive multi-omic single-cell atlas study with exceptional scope: 220 donors, 13 age groups, 5 modalities, 1.2M cells. Three well-defined contributions (T cell trajectories, cytotoxic B cells, immune aging clock) are each supported by quantitative data. The study design with flow cytometry validation and cross-modal consistency is rigorous. Methods are detailed and reproducible. Minor weaknesses: single-population cohort limitation is acknowledged; the immune aging clock uses ridge regression which may underfit complex age-immune relationships; cytotoxic B cell functional characterization is descriptive rather than mechanistic.
